Title: Overexpression of a Tonoplast Malate Transporter Gene Leads to Enhanced Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Apple

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit peel red coloration; antioxidant/stress-related flavonoid function
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ALMT9-type tonoplast malate transporter; aluminium-activated malate transporter family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in apple fruit; native expression in peel/bulk fruit responds negatively to Ma1 overexpression feedback via MdMYB73
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in fruit peel during colour development and in transiently infiltrated peel
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages from 50 DBH to harvest; light-sensitive peel assays in Zestar; transient agroinfiltration
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of Ma1 enhances peel anthocyanin biosynthesis, whereas Ma1 RNAi decreases anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stable cMa1 overexpression in Royal Gala increased red peel area and anthocyanin concentration across fruit development. 2. Transient overexpression of Ma1α, Ma1β, or genomic Ma1 in Zestar peel increased anthocyanin, while Ma1-RNAi decreased it. 3. Ma1 overexpression increased MdDFR, MdANS, and MdUFGT expression and decreased MdMYB73 expression, linking malate transport to anthocyanin regulation through feedback on MdMYB73.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression lines; transient overexpression; RNAi suppression; anthocyanin quantification; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ma1 is primarily known for fruit acidity control via vacuolar malate transport; in this paper it acts upstream of anthocyanin regulation indirectly through feedback downregulation of MdMYB73. Alternative splicing generates Ma1α and Ma1β; both transcripts were functionally considered in transient assays.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: cMa1-OE fruits had about one-third of wild-type titratable acidity and slightly higher soluble solids at harvest; altered acidity is a major quality tradeoff
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for coupling peel coloration and acidity traits, but deployment requires caution because cMa1 coding-sequence overexpression lowers fruit acidity. Genomic Ma1 or isoform-balanced engineering may better tune both acidity and coloration.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins to form stable anthocyanin glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant; key control step supported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; induced in cMa1-OE and MdMYB73-RNAi peels; reduced by MdMYB73 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit peel coloration stage near harvest; transient agroinfiltration in Zestar and Royal Gala peel
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced MdUFGT expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation; RNAi suppression blocks red colour development and blocks the promotive effect of MdMYB73-RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdUFGT expression was elevated in cMa1-OE fruit peel and in MdMYB73-RNAi tissues. 2. MdMYB73 binds the MdUFGT promoter at seven MYB sites and represses its transcription. 3. MdUFGT-RNAi reduced anthocyanin accumulation and abolished the anthocyanin increase caused by MdMYB73-RNAi, placing MdUFGT downstream of MdMYB73. 4. MdUFGT overexpression increased anthocyanin synthesis and MdMYB1 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression; transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; anthocyanin profiling; Y1H; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdMYB73 and MdMYB1 bind the same seven MYB sites in the promoter with opposite regulatory outputs; MdUFGT may feed forward to enhance MdMYB1 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for boosting apple peel anthocyanins; likely most effective when combined with reduced MdMYB73 repression or enhanced MdMYB1 activation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdUFGT promoter (2000 bp) assayed in Y1H and luciferase systems

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: red peel pigmentation; antioxidant/stress-associated flavonoid function
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor, subgroup 22 MYB, EAR motif-containing
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Negative feedback from Ma1/cMa1 overexpression; fruit developmental context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and malate accumulation/vacuolar acidification
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of MdUFGT and transcriptional activation of Ma1; competitive promoter binding with MdMYB1 at MdUFGT promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator linking fruit acidity control to anthocyanin biosynthesis; release from MdMYB73 repression is essential for elevated MdUFGT expression and anthocyanin accumulation in cMa1-OE fruit
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction with MdbHLH3 detected by Y2H; competition is with MdMYB1 for shared cis-elements rather than bHLH binding
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in feedback loop: Ma1 overexpression suppresses MdMYB73 expression; MdUFGT may indirectly feed forward to increase MdMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in fruit peel during colour development; decreased in cMa1-OE peel; reduced in RNAi lines and increased by Ma1-RNAi
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Fruit peel and bulk fruit; also implicated in calli/leaves in related assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages; transient RNAi/overexpression in peel; stable RNAi lines; luciferase assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppressing MdMYB73 increases anthocyanin biosynthesis, whereas overexpressing MdMYB73 decreases anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB73 transcript levels are reduced in cMa1-OE fruits and inversely correlate with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Stable and transient RNAi of MdMYB73 increased anthocyanin and MdUFGT expression, while overexpression had the opposite effect. 3. MdMYB73 directly binds all seven MYB sites in the MdUFGT promoter and represses promoter activity. 4. MdMYB73 competes with MdMYB1 for MdUFGT promoter occupancy, antagonizing anthocyanin activation. 5. MdMYB73 is bifunctional, activating Ma1 for acidity control while repressing MdUFGT for anthocyanin synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable RNAi transgenics; transient RNAi and overexpression; RT-qPCR; anthocyanin quantification; Y1H; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; factorial combinational assays with MdMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdMYB1 (functional competition at MdUFGT promoter); Ma1 (feedback relationship); MdbHLH3 no interaction detected
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional TF with opposite effects on different target genes. Repression likely involves its EAR motif, but co-repressors were not identified.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lowers Ma1 expression and thus can alter fruit acidity; MdMYB73 is also implicated in abiotic and biotic stress tolerance in cited related work, suggesting possible stress-related tradeoffs if suppressed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to raise peel anthocyanin, but edits should preserve desirable acidity and stress tolerance. Tissue-specific or fruit peel-specific reduction may be preferable to constitutive suppression.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: red peel pigmentation; antioxidant/stress-associated flavonoid function
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit coloration developmental program; indirectly enhanced when MdUFGT is elevated
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; competes with MdMYB73 for the same MYB cis-elements in the MdUFGT promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key transcriptional activator of anthocyanin synthesis whose access to the MdUFGT promoter is increased when MdMYB73 repression is relieved
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Known from cited prior work to interact with MdbHLH3; in this paper competition with MdMYB73 occurs at DNA-binding level
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: MdUFGT overexpression increased MdMYB1 expression, suggesting feedforward regulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; increased in cMa1-OE and MdMYB73-RNAi contexts; unchanged by transient MdMYB1 overexpression with respect to MdMYB73/Ma1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit peel near harvest; transient overexpression/RNAi combinations; luciferase assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis through activation of MdUFGT; its effect is antagonized by MdMYB73 competition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1 expression was higher in cMa1-OE peels and in some contexts of increased anthocyanin accumulation. 2. MdMYB1 binds all seven MYB sites in the MdUFGT promoter. 3. Combined assays showed that MdMYB73 antagonizes MdMYB1-driven MdUFGT expression and anthocyanin accumulation by competition for promoter binding. 4. MdMYB1 overexpression increased anthocyanin synthesis but did not alter MdMYB73 or Ma1 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; transient overexpression/RNAi factorial assays in peel; RT-qPCR; anthocyanin quantification; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdMYB73 (competitive relationship on promoter); MdbHLH3 (reported previously, not newly validated here)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to mechanism, MdMYB1 is not the primary new discovery of the study; included because it is experimentally interrogated in Results as a core anthocyanin regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for anthocyanin enhancement, especially when combined with relief of MdMYB73 repression or elevated MdUFGT.
